page: Vultr vbm-72c-480gb-gh200-gpu is a bare metal server configured with the NVIDIA Grace Hopper architecture. It features a 72-core NVIDIA Grace CPU based on the arm64 Neoverse V2 architecture running at 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 480 GB of system memory, providing approximately 6.67 GB of RAM per physical core.

This server integrates a single NVIDIA GH200 GPU from the Hopper family, featuring 96 GB of dedicated VRAM. For local storage, the configuration includes a 960 GB NVMe SSD. The server also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The unified memory architecture of the Grace Hopper superchip facilitates high-bandwidth data transfer between the CPU and GPU. From a cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal instance provides a dense consolidation of high-performance ARM cores and Hopper GPU resources on a single board, reducing latency and overhead compared to multi-socket configurations. This hardware profile is designed for workloads such as large language model inference, deep learning training, high-performance computing, and data-intensive scientific simulations.
description: Vultr vbm-72c-480gb-gh200-gpu is a bare metal server utilizing the NVIDIA Grace Hopper architecture. It features a 72-core NVIDIA Grace CPU based on the arm64 Neoverse V2 architecture operating at 3.1 GHz, paired with 480 GB of system memory. The server integrates a single NVIDIA GH200 Hopper GPU with 96 GB of VRAM, alongside a 960 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. By combining dedicated ARM-based processing cores with a high-capacity Hopper GPU on a single platform, this server delivers high resource density and efficient data exchange between the CPU and GPU. This configuration is optimized for demanding workloads including artificial intelligence training, large language model inference, high-performance computing, and complex data analytics.

tagline: An arm64 bare metal platform combining a seventy-two core processor with an integrated high-capacity graphics accelerator for intensive parallel computing.
